body.title: Privacy guard 'stored_locally_only:CONTACT_*' fires as raw output instead of being silently handled
body.result: Added _PRIVACY_SENTINEL_RE to _is_raw_tool_leak — catches stored_locally_only:CONTACT_* and stored_locally|google_error:* before reply reaches web_server.send. 11 tests. Follow-up: metaphor-guard on contact habit firing is inhibitory-primitive territory, not this ticket.
body.description:
AUTHORED BY IGOR-WILD-0001 on 2026-04-13. Claude filed this on Igor's behalf because Igor's emit-to-queue path didn't actually land — see T-igor-emit-action-confabulation for the meta-bug.

Igor's words: 'privacy guard fires as raw output rather than being silently handled, and it halts the conversation thread. Fix approach: intercept at the output layer before text reaches the channel, substitute either nothing or a graceful continuation, never let a privacy sentinel become user-visible noise.'

Observed instance from 4/9 transcript: Akien said 'the person is in the database' (metaphorical statement about architecture). Contact-storage habit pattern-matched on 'person'+'database' keywords without checking that no actual contact was being introduced. Habit emitted 'stored_locally_only:CONTACT_375375DD48' as Igor's full reply. The privacy sentinel was visible to the user instead of being intercepted.

FIX: output layer should detect privacy sentinels before they reach the channel send path. When detected, either (a) suppress entirely and continue with a normal LLM reply, or (b) replace with a graceful continuation phrase that doesn't reveal internal state. Never let the sentinel reach the user.

SECONDARY ISSUE: the contact-storage habit fires on metaphors. Should also check that text actually contains a contact-introduction context, not just keyword matches. Possibly route through a 'is this literal vs figurative' guard before firing privacy-sensitive habits.
